hero-role: Data Engineer · Business Analyst — Finance & Risk
hero-tagline: Hybrid technical / functional profile, at the interface between business and IT. Available for full-remote consulting mandates across Western Europe.
about-p1: Data Engineer and Business Analyst with three years of experience at the interface between business teams and information systems — across regulated banking and international industrial environments. Hybrid technical and functional profile: equally comfortable designing an ETL pipeline and writing its functional specifications, building an ML model and presenting its results to non-technical stakeholders.
about-p2: I run projects end-to-end — from requirements gathering and workshop facilitation through to UAT, production hand-off, and steering-committee reporting — with a strong focus on data quality and deliverable robustness.
about-p3: Currently on a mission within the automotive-leasing subsidiary of a major European banking group, where I pivot between functional and technical work on Basel III Standard Approach frameworks, NDoD regulatory reporting, and credit risk segmentation — primarily in Oracle SQL/PL-SQL.
about-p4: Engineering degree in Data Engineering from Télécom Saint-Étienne. Currently pursuing the FRM certification (GARP). Consulting target: full-remote mandates for Western European clients in Finance, Risk, or regulatory data.
experience-1-employer: Solutec — on mission at the automotive-leasing subsidiary of a major European banking group
experience-1-bullet1: Designed and shipped an Oracle SQL processing engine covering default-event detection, cure identification and prudential portfolio segmentation — complex business logic translated into auditable data transformations (NDoD · EBA / Basel III / CRR)
experience-1-bullet2: Requirements gathering across Risk, Finance and Compliance teams; detailed functional specifications (business rules, data dictionaries, ETL flows, test cases)
experience-1-bullet3: Facilitation of scoping and prioritisation workshops (MOA / MOE liaison); Jira backlog ownership, user-story tracking from spec to production
experience-1-bullet4: Coordination of UAT campaigns (unit and functional tests), anomaly traceability and correction follow-up
experience-1-bullet5: Analytical assets for credit-risk KPI monitoring; data-quality anomaly detection and corrective flows; regular reporting to the steering committee
experience-2-employer: Lennox EMEA — listed industrial group, EMEA scope
experience-2-bullet1: Led an end-to-end BI migration (legacy → Power BI / SSRS) across European entities — requirements, architecture, deployment, training — +30% cross-department reporting efficiency
experience-2-bullet2: Built an XGBoost predictive model (75% precision) integrated into the S&OP process; results and recommendations presented to business stakeholders — +25% forecast reliability
experience-2-bullet3: Designed Python / SQL pipelines automating multi-entity financial reporting flows
experience-2-bullet4: Cost & margin analytics enabling quarterly removal of 10+ unprofitable projects; dynamic production / sales / finance dashboards
projects-intro: Three projects in build — one per month. GitHub and live links will appear here as each one ships.
project-1-desc: A production-grade PL/SQL credit scoring engine built on Oracle XE, simulating a synthetic automotive leasing portfolio. Implements the Basel III Standard Approach for credit risk capital requirement calculation, including risk weight assignment, exposure segmentation, and RWA computation. Stored procedures, control tables, and reconciliation views included.
project-2-desc: A Power BI dashboard built on public EBA (European Banking Authority) data. Compares major European banks across key prudential ratios: CET1, Tier 1, LCR, NSFR. Designed to demonstrate regulatory data literacy and visual analytics skills in a Finance & Risk context. Downloadable .pbix file provided.
project-3-desc: An end-to-end credit scoring model built with XGBoost on a synthetic loan portfolio, featuring full SHAP explainability for individual predictions. The model is deployed as an interactive Streamlit application: input borrower characteristics and receive a risk score with a breakdown of contributing factors. Designed to bridge ML modelling with regulatory explainability requirements.
footer-role: Data Engineer · Business Analyst — Finance & Risk
colophon-set-in: Set in Newsreader, Inter & IBM Plex Mono
